title: Klikk lease-AI clause chunk
description: Schema for a single clause-corpus YAML file under backend/apps/leases/lease_law_corpus/clauses/**/*.yml. The clauses corpus is owned by the lease-AI workstream; statutes / case_law / pitfalls live in apps.legal_rag (see content/cto/centralised-legal-rag-store-plan.md §8 step 6). See lease-ai-agent-architecture.md §6.2.

type.description: Always 'clause' in this corpus. statutes / case_law / pitfalls live in apps.legal_rag.

version.description: Schema-content version. Bump on substantive edits; the id suffix must match.

content_hash.description: sha256 of the canonical YAML at index time. Set by the indexer (manage.py reindex_lease_corpus). Authors do NOT hand-edit this; it is checked but tolerated as absent in source YAML.

clause_title.description: Human-readable title shown in the Drafter's clause-picker.

clause_body.description: The clause text. May contain {{ merge_field }} placeholders; every placeholder name must appear in merge_fields_used.

topic_tags.description: Topic tags used by the Drafter's Front Door push pattern. Lower-snake-case.

related_citations.description: FK strings to apps.legal_rag concept_ids (e.g. 'rha-s5-3-f-deposit-interest-bearing-account'). Validation here is structural only; cross-reference resolution lives in apps.legal_rag.

applicability.description: Filters used by query_clauses() metadata search.

merge_fields_used.description: Every {{ name }} placeholder inside clause_body must appear here. Empty list is legal for a static clause with no merge fields.

disclaimers.description: Free-text disclaimers shown next to the clause in admin UI / surfaced to Reviewer.

citation_confidence.description: Mirrors the canonical citation map's HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW. LOW chunks set legal_provisional true and are excluded from Drafter default retrieval (see architecture §6.5.1).

legal_provisional.description: True when the underlying statute citation is LOW confidence in the canonical map; Reviewer refuses external release without override.

effective_from.description: ISO date the clause text became effective.

last_reviewed_at.description: ISO date of the last human review.
